channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How does the Nintendo Switch 2 stack up against gaming handhelds like the Steam Deck and Rog Ally X? Find out in our latest podcast episode
item.description: On the latest episode of the TechRadar Podcast, we discuss the Switch 2, tarriffs, the rumored iPhone 17 Air and Microsoft's 50th birthday celebrations.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Reddit Answers Gets Google Gemini Integration: A Game-Changer for Users?
item.description: Reddit Answers, the upcoming AI platform, is getting a major update with Google Gemini integration. This move adds credibility to Reddit Answers and may change how users interact with the platform.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Drones are the future of cybercrime
item.description: The Ukrainian military is teaching the world how to creatively use drones for battlefield offense and defense. Ukraine has built a vast arsenal of drones customized for precision strikes, reconnaissance, and kamikaze missions. But this innovation also extends to cybersecurity offense and defense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Trump's trade war with China will hit tech prices
item.description: President Trump’s escalating trade war with China is putting a spotlight on the various technology companies and products poised to feel the ripple effects of the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: SAP customers on Business Suite: New strategy, same old concerns
item.description: SAP in February dusted off its legacy on-prem Business Suite label and resurrected it for the cloud era under a new strategic banner “Business Unleashed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gigabit NBN plans are cheaper, faster and better than ever – these are 4 I'd recommend
item.description: Fast speeds, low costs and plenty of extra perks, NBN 1000 plans are becoming increasingly more attractive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Instagram bans livestreaming for kids without parental consent
item.description: Meta announced on Tuesday new “built-in protections” for its Instagram Teen Accounts, including a new requirement for parents to consent before children under 16 can go “live” on the platform or unblur nudity in images they receive in direct messages.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The Last of Us season 2 won't be the hugely successful Max TV show's final entry as HBO officially announces its third season
item.description: HBO confirmed The Last of Us season 3 is in the works just days before the show's second season airs.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft says it's “slowing or pausing” some data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Google Cloud introduces cloud app design center
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Application Design Center, a service that helps platform administrators and developers design, deploy, and manage applications on the Google Cloud Platform. Application Design Center is in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Four paradoxes of software development
item.description: Civil engineers can rightfully claim that no two bridges are exactly alike. However, bridges share many known characteristics, and the materials they are built with have known characteristics. Building bridges involves many known knowns and not as many unknown unknowns as one might think.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DoubleClickjacking hack turns double-clicks into account takeovers
item.description: Double-clicking is something we all do, often without giving it a second thought. But it could be giving hackers permission to do something dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan begins to lay out technology roadmap
item.description: He’s only been on the job for two weeks, but newly appointed Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan gave a major speech at a partner conference this week where he said the ailing company needs to get its act together, and he doesn’t want customers to hold back on their criticism.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, Fox 5
